title: Ma al-istifhamiyya and Ma al-nafiya: Differentiating Question Words and Negation in Arabic
summary: The session introduces two uses of 'ma' in Arabic: ma al-istifhamiyya (interrogative) and ma al-nafiya (negation). The teacher explains how context determines whether 'ma' functions as a question word or negates a statement. Students practice examples involving possession, pronouns, and forming negative sentences using 'ma'.
key_points: Ma الistiHAMiyya is used for asking questions (e.g., مَا هَذَا؟ - What is this?).
Ma anafiya negates statements (e.g., مَا عِندِي كِتَاب - I do not have a book).
Context determines the role of 'ma'; answer words like 'la' are used for responses to questions.
Pronouns with possession: Using li versus 'indī ('عندِي' for objects, 'li' for humans and specific contexts).
Examples practice with different nouns (car, book) and personal pronouns (my, your, his, her).
